romania.title: Cristian Mungiu, la Cannes 2026: „Nu sunt aici pentru a face pe placul cuiva. În cinema trebuie să îţi asumi riscuri”
romania.summary: Prin intermediul celui mai recent lungmetraj al său, „Fjord", selectat în competiţia oficială a Festivalului de la Cannes, Cristian Mungiu pune sub semnul întrebării abuzurile comise în numele ideologiilor progresiste, fără să se teamă că stârneşte dezbateri. „Cinemaul trebuie să rămână polemic", a declarat regizorul român, recompensat cu trofeul Palme d'Or în 2007.

romania.title: „Danaida” lui Brâncuşi a devenit a doua cea mai scumpă sculptură din istorie, după licitația în care s-a vândut la un preț record
romania.summary: „Danaida” de Constantin Brâncuşi, vândută la Christie's pentru 107,6 milioane de dolari, a devenit a doua cea mai scumpă sculptură din istoria licitaţiilor.

romania.title: Zentiva susține biodiversitatea prin adoptarea a 20 de stupi, contribuind la protejarea a aproximativ 1,2 milioane de albine
romania.summary: Grupul de companii Zentiva din România, unul dintre cei mai importanți producători de medicamente generice pe plan local, își extinde angajamentul pentru protejarea mediului, prin continuarea implicării în programul „Adopt… Articolul Zentiva susține biodiversitatea prin adoptarea a 20 de stupi, contribuind la protejarea a aproximativ 1,2 milioane de albine apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.summary: Ediția de anul trecut a Festivalului Național de Teatru s-a deschis un show inedit marca Radu Afrim, un spectacol-concert care a propus, pe fondul unor vremuri tulburi, o întoarcere la… Articolul GRO(O)VE sau Once is not enough apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: 694 de copii vaccinați și peste 19.000 identificați ca restanțieri în cadrul campaniei naționale ROR „Copil vaccinat = copil protejat
romania.summary: București, 19 mai 2026 – În cadrul campaniei naționale de promovare a vaccinării „Copil vaccinat = copil protejat”, desfășurată în perioada 20–30 aprilie 2026, au fost identificați 19.528 de copii… Articolul 694 de copii vaccinați și peste 19.000 identificați ca restanțieri în cadrul campaniei naționale ROR „Copil vaccinat = copil protejat apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.summary: Bee Green, atelierul de idei și invenții pentru natură, invită copiii și adolescenții din Timișoara la o experiență educațională de weekend în grădina urbană GreenFeel, cu observații în natură, experimente,… Articolul Bee Green @ GreenFeel: copiii devin cercetători juniori ai polenizatorilor urbani apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.summary: Veşti bune pentru toţi românii care s-au săturat să îi îmbogăţească pe instalatori! O componentă esenţială din dotarea oricărui specialist în ţevi şi scurgeri, crăpătura de cur, e acum la vânzare la Lidl pentru doar 39 de lei şi 99 de bani. Potrivit producătorilor, crăpătura de cur e dotată cu bretele şi buzunare, putând fi […] The post De ce să mai dai bani la instalatori? Articolul Gândirea civică – concertul unei generații: povestea lui Ionuț, un tânăr în fața provocărilor cu care se confruntă tinerii de astăzi apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.summary: Un bucureștean care se plictisea a găsit o metodă extrem de simpatică de a-și ocupa timpul liber. El a construit o imensă capcană umană, inspirată de una pentru șobolani, după care a plasat în apropierea ei mai multe cutii de pantofi pe post de momeală. Așa cum era de așteptat, nu a trecut mult până […] The post Un șugubăț l-a momit pe Grindeanu cu mai multe cutii de pantofi și l-a prins într-o capcană appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: 😂 Cocalarii cu BMW, revoltați că PMB vrea să cumpere 100 de tramvaie: „Abia avem noi loc pe șine!”
romania.summary: Cocalarii bucureșteni s-au revoltat după ce Primăria a anunțat că vrea să cumpere în jur de 100 de tramvaie noi. Ei spun că și în prezent condițiile de trafic sunt dificile, însă cu încă 100 de tramvaie pe șine ele vor deveni de-a dreptul dezastruoase. „În mod normal ar trebui eliminate din tramvaiele care circulă […] The post Cocalarii cu BMW, revoltați că PMB vrea să cumpere 100 de tramvaie: „Abia avem noi loc pe șine!” appeared first on Times New Roman .

Articolul Schimbările mari încep cu pași mici: povestea lui Robert și drumul lui către advocacy la nivel internațional apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.summary: Alexandru are 10 ani, locuiește într-un sat din județul Giurgiu și este elev în clasa a III‑a. Este un copil cu... Articolul Visul lui Alexandru începe cu o minge apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.summary: Programul național Telefonul Vârstnicului – 0800 460 001 Sprijinul media a venit de la Spot TV – Pro TV, TVR, Antena 1, Antena 3 CNN, Antena Stars, Prima TV, Prima News, Pro Cinema, Pro Arena, Acasă, Acasă Gold, Trinitas TV, Speranța TV, Antena 3 Deva,... romania.title: Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București!
romania.summary: Cine suntem noi și de cine avem nevoie? Suntem echipa de la Telefonul Vârstnicului, serviciu social licențiat, singura linie telefonică gratuită și confidențială, accesibilă la nivel național, dedicată persoanelor vârstnice, ce oferă consiliere socială și alinarea singurătății. În fiecare an suntem sunați de peste 5,000... Post-ul Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București! apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

romania.summary: București, aprilie 2026 – Într-o lume în care cererea de hrană crește accelerat, iar presiunea asupra resurselor naturale este tot... Articolul Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.summary: Locație: București | Nivel carieră: Experiență de minimum 1 an Candidatul Ideal: Ești tu vocea de care au nevoie? Un copil are nevoie de iubire, siguranță și încredere pentru a... The post Psiholog appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

romania.title: „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase.
romania.summary: Într-o casă modestă din județul Vaslui, unde mirosul de lemne arse se amestecă cu zgomotul pașilor mici de copii și... Articolul „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase. apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.title: De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță
romania.summary: În timp ce pentru mulți români Paștele este sărbătoarea revederii și a meselor în familie, pentru mulți vârstnici singuri aceste zile sunt printre cele mai grele din an. Singurătatea doare, lipsurile și sentimentul de abandon se simt mai apăsat tocmai atunci când toți ceilalți par... Post-ul De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

medical.title: After the COVID‑19 pandemic, thousands more kindergarteners faced developmental challenges
medical.summary: Children with special needs often experience a range of early developmental challenges that can affect their readiness for learning and their participation in school.

medical.title: A new way to strengthen the body's defense against respiratory viruses
medical.summary: Researchers have discovered a new method to boost the body's natural ability to fight respiratory viruses, including respiratory syncytial virus (RSV), one of the leading causes of severe lung infections worldwide.

medical.title: Dementia risk factors may depend on which country you live in
medical.summary: A new study led by Curtin University has revealed millions of dementia cases across the Western Pacific Region could potentially be prevented by implementing country-specific strategies to address key risk factors such as education access, obesity and mental health.

medical.title: Benefits of using a Bayesian statistical framework for replicating research
medical.summary: The "replication crisis" refers to a problem in the sciences where findings from previous experiments don't hold up when studies are repeated. It is a particular issue for those in the behavioral sciences and experimental psychology; studies that should hold up, don't, and researchers have struggled with the vast task of both confirming what is known and trying to glean new and accurate information. The concerns ramped up in the 2010s, when repeated experiments to corroborate published psychological studies ended up failing to replicate, adding to concerns about how studies are conducted and published in the first place.

medical.title: Elderly people are more sexually active than most people think
medical.summary: Sexuality is an important part of life—even when we grow old. The idea that desire disappears with age is a myth that needs to be debunked, argues a psychologist who has researched older adults' sex lives.

medical.title: Protecting the brain: How clumps of protein might actually be saving your cells
medical.summary: What if the very structures we thought were destroying the brain are actually trying to save it? A new study reveals that protein clumps, long considered toxic markers of diseases like Huntington's, act as a vital "quarantine" system to keep neurons alive. By identifying the protein ATF3 as the master switch that builds these shields, researchers have uncovered a natural defense mechanism that shifts the focus from destroying these clumps to strengthening the brain's internal armor. This discovery offers a fresh perspective on how we might one day treat neurodegenerative disorders by working with the body's own survival strategies.

medical.title: BMI alone does not fully capture health risks linked to obesity, new study finds
medical.summary: Obesity is commonly diagnosed using BMI, but this approach has several limitations. Researchers at Lund University and AstraZeneca show that integrating measurements such as body fat percentage and waist circumference captures disease risks missed by BMI alone.

medical.title: 'Jumping gene' helps explain elevated pancreatic cancer risk in French-Canadians
medical.summary: Researchers at McGill University have discovered a centuries-old genetic mutation that helps to explain why some French‑Canadians in Quebec are at an elevated risk of pancreatic cancer. Until quite recently, standard genetic tests have not been able to identify this "jumping gene" cause.

medical.title: Therapy at your fingertips: New study finds AI could transform mental health care
medical.summary: A new study from Reichman University, published in JAMA Network Open, has found that an AI-based conversational support platform can significantly reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression, form a meaningful therapeutic alliance with users, and provide round-the-clock emotional support. The study is titled "Efficacy of a Conversational AI Agent for Psychiatric Symptoms and Digital Therapeutic Alliance: A Randomized Clinical Trial."

science.title: New chip offers way to make use of quantum system 'imperfections'
science.summary: Quantum technologies promise powerful new kinds of computers, giving scientists new tools to mimic and explore nature at its tiniest scales. At those levels, everything in nature—from atoms and electrons to light itself—follows the strange rules of quantum mechanics. But the real world is never perfectly clean: Signals fade, energy leaks away and systems pick up noise from their surroundings.

science.title: Does emotional intelligence stop us from being rude? Here's what the science says
science.summary: We often assume that how we respond to a rude comment says something stable about us: our personality, our culture, even the language we are speaking. If someone reacts calmly, we presume they are a patient person. If they snap back, we might assume they are short-tempered.

science.title: Imperfect polymer sequences still control protein function, revealing new design rules
science.summary: What happens when a scientific problem seems too complex to solve precisely, yet understanding it could reshape how researchers design new materials and medicines? For decades, much of the polymer science community has relied on a "good enough" approach to a stubborn problem: binding a polymer to a protein in a precise way that reliably controls how the protein behaves.

science.title: This German dialect leaves AI baffled, exposing a digital language blind spot
science.summary: How well do language models understand Meenzerisch, the dialect spoken in the German city of Mainz? A research team led by Johannes Gutenberg University Mainz (JGU) has now investigated this question for the first time. Meenzerisch still shapes regional language culture today and is known across Germany through the satirical carnival speeches in the famous Mainz Fastnacht tradition. The study's findings, recently presented at the 2026 Language Resources and Evaluation Conference (LREC) in Palma de Mallorca, show that current Artificial Intelligence (AI) models have great difficulty understanding the dialect correctly.

science.title: How a 4,000-year-old city defied history's 'rules' by becoming more equal as it became more successful
science.summary: For decades, historians have generally agreed that the progress of small villages as they evolved into cities came at the price of widening inequality. A small group of leaders, kings and priests, would inevitably seize control of the wealth and the gap between rich and poor would grow.

science.title: Molecular net boosts the power of natural biopesticides
science.summary: Scientists at VIB and Vrije Universiteit Brussel have uncovered a previously unknown mechanism that helps a widely used biological pesticide become more effective. The study, published in Nature Communications, reveals how bacteria produce ultra-strong protein fibers that form a molecular net, trapping infectious spores and toxins into a sticky film that enhances their ability to kill insect pests.

science.title: Economists solve a mystery involving international trade and competition from China
science.summary: Economists have identified—and resolved—a seeming paradox regarding how competition from China affects the price and volume of products that are exported from other countries into the United States. The findings shed new light on the complex dynamics of international trade and how the effects of trade competition vary drastically for poor nations compared to their wealthy counterparts.

science.title: How a shifting Nile landscape shaped the rise of the ancient empire of Kush in Sudan
science.summary: When I first became co-director of an archaeological project at Jebel Barkal in northern Sudan in 2018, I was amazed by the site's pyramids, temples and palaces. It had been an urban center in the ancient empire of Kush, which dominated the Nile Valley off and on for more than 2,000 years, from 2000 B.C.E. to 350 C.E.

science.title: Grokipedia selectively draws on more-right leaning news sources, says new study
science.summary: A large-scale analysis of Grokipedia, the world's first AI-written encyclopedia, has found that while many Grokipedia articles closely resemble their Wikipedia counterparts, a substantial subset diverged markedly in style, sourcing and political leaning.

science.title: Moon-Venus Conjunction
science.summary: The Moon and Venus, center, are seen in conjunction above the Washington Monument, Monday, May 18, 2026, as viewed from the Mary W. Jackson NASA Headquarters Building in Washington. The Moon and Venus look close together because they line up from our point of view on Earth. In reality, they are separated by millions of […]

environment.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
environment.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized features, such as stubby arms and ant-eating adaptations. Weighing under two pounds, the dinosaur is one of the smallest known from South America.

environment.title: Scientists finally solve the mystery of yeast’s tiny centromeres
environment.summary: Scientists have uncovered how brewer’s yeast developed its unusually tiny centromeres, the DNA regions that guide chromosome separation during cell division. By studying related yeast species, researchers found centromeres that appear to represent evolutionary halfway points. These structures seem to have formed from retrotransposons—mobile “jumping genes” in the genome. The discovery shows how DNA once considered genomic junk can be transformed into essential chromosome machinery.

environment.title: Light-guided evolution creates proteins that can switch, sense, and compute
environment.summary: Researchers have created a method called optovolution that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. By engineering yeast cells so their survival depended on proteins switching states at the right time, scientists could rapidly select the best-performing variants. The technique produced new light-sensitive proteins that respond to different colors and improved optogenetic systems. It even evolved a protein that behaves like a tiny logic gate, activating genes only when two signals are present.

environment.title: Scientists discover hidden species among Borneo’s “fanged frogs”
environment.summary: DNA is revealing that many animals once thought to be a single species may actually be several hidden ones. But research on Bornean fanged frogs shows the line between species can be blurry—an important challenge when deciding what wildlife needs protection most.

environment.title: Lake Coatepeque
environment.summary: Set amid El Salvador’s modern, active volcanic landscape, tranquil blue waters fill a caldera formed by ancient eruptions. The post Lake Coatepeque appeared first on NASA Science .

environment.title: Scientists stunned to find signs of ancient life in a place no one expected
environment.summary: While exploring ancient seabeds in Morocco, scientists discovered strange wrinkle-like textures in deep-water sediments that shouldn’t have been there. These structures are usually made by sunlight-loving microbial mats in shallow waters. But the rocks formed far below the reach of light, suggesting a different explanation. Evidence points to chemosynthetic microbes—organisms powered by chemical reactions—creating the mats in the dark depths of an ancient ocean.

environment.title: Scientists finally reveal why mint feels cold
environment.summary: Scientists have revealed how the body’s microscopic cold sensor, TRPM8, detects both chilly temperatures and the cooling effect of menthol. The discovery finally shows how the sensation of “cool” works at the molecular level—and could inspire new treatments for pain and eye disorders.

environment.title: Bird droppings helped build one of ancient Peru’s most powerful kingdoms
environment.summary: New research suggests seabird guano helped transform the Chincha Kingdom into one of the most prosperous societies in ancient Peru. Chemical clues in centuries-old maize show farmers fertilized their crops with guano gathered from nearby islands, dramatically boosting yields in the desert landscape. The resulting agricultural surplus fueled trade, population growth, and regional influence.

mix_items.title: Cristian Mungiu, la Cannes 2026: „Nu sunt aici pentru a face pe placul cuiva. În cinema trebuie să îţi asumi riscuri”
mix_items.summary: Prin intermediul celui mai recent lungmetraj al său, „Fjord", selectat în competiţia oficială a Festivalului de la Cannes, Cristian Mungiu pune sub semnul întrebării abuzurile comise în numele ideologiilor progresiste, fără să se teamă că stârneşte dezbateri. „Cinemaul trebuie să rămână polemic", a declarat regizorul român, recompensat cu trofeul Palme d'Or în 2007.

mix_items.summary: Locație: București Fii adultul care poate schimba viitorul unui copil Dacă ai răbdare, empatie și îți dorești un job cu adevărat valoros, în care fiecare zi contează pentru dezvoltarea unor... The post Psihopedagog/Pedagog appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.summary: Robert are 18 ani și este reprezentant al copiilor în Consiliul Consultativ al Copiilor și Tinerilor din cadrul World Vision România. Prin... Articolul Schimbările mari încep cu pași mici: povestea lui Robert și drumul lui către advocacy la nivel internațional apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.summary: Locație: București Contract: Part time (remote) Credem în cauza SOS Satele Copiilor și știm că fiecare copil merită să crească într-o familie iubitoare. De 30 de ani SOS Satele Copiilor... The post Agent telemarketing (team leader) appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.summary: Alexandru are 10 ani, locuiește într-un sat din județul Giurgiu și este elev în clasa a III‑a. Este un copil cu... Articolul Visul lui Alexandru începe cu o minge apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.summary: Programul național Telefonul Vârstnicului – 0800 460 001 Sprijinul media a venit de la Spot TV – Pro TV, TVR, Antena 1, Antena 3 CNN, Antena Stars, Prima TV, Prima News, Pro Cinema, Pro Arena, Acasă, Acasă Gold, Trinitas TV, Speranța TV, Antena 3 Deva,... Post-ul SPRIJIN MEDIA OFERIT PROGRAMELOR FUNDAȚIEI REGALE ÎN ANUL 2025 apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: New chip offers way to make use of quantum system 'imperfections'
mix_items.summary: Quantum technologies promise powerful new kinds of computers, giving scientists new tools to mimic and explore nature at its tiniest scales. At those levels, everything in nature—from atoms and electrons to light itself—follows the strange rules of quantum mechanics. But the real world is never perfectly clean: Signals fade, energy leaks away and systems pick up noise from their surroundings.

mix_items.title: Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București!
mix_items.summary: Cine suntem noi și de cine avem nevoie? Suntem echipa de la Telefonul Vârstnicului, serviciu social licențiat, singura linie telefonică gratuită și confidențială, accesibilă la nivel național, dedicată persoanelor vârstnice, ce oferă consiliere socială și alinarea singurătății. În fiecare an suntem sunați de peste 5,000... Post-ul Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București! apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas
mix_items.summary: Un drum construit prin educație Simona este elevă în clasa a XI-a, la specializarea Tehnician în industria alimentară, la un... Articolul Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Does emotional intelligence stop us from being rude? Here's what the science says
mix_items.summary: We often assume that how we respond to a rude comment says something stable about us: our personality, our culture, even the language we are speaking. If someone reacts calmly, we presume they are a patient person. If they snap back, we might assume they are short-tempered.

mix_items.title: Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale
mix_items.summary: București, aprilie 2026 – Într-o lume în care cererea de hrană crește accelerat, iar presiunea asupra resurselor naturale este tot... Articolul Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Psiholog
mix_items.summary: Locație: București | Nivel carieră: Experiență de minimum 1 an Candidatul Ideal: Ești tu vocea de care au nevoie? Un copil are nevoie de iubire, siguranță și încredere pentru a... The post Psiholog appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.title: Imperfect polymer sequences still control protein function, revealing new design rules
mix_items.summary: What happens when a scientific problem seems too complex to solve precisely, yet understanding it could reshape how researchers design new materials and medicines? For decades, much of the polymer science community has relied on a "good enough" approach to a stubborn problem: binding a polymer to a protein in a precise way that reliably controls how the protein behaves.

mix_items.title: „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase.
mix_items.summary: Într-o casă modestă din județul Vaslui, unde mirosul de lemne arse se amestecă cu zgomotul pașilor mici de copii și... Articolul „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase. apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță
mix_items.summary: În timp ce pentru mulți români Paștele este sărbătoarea revederii și a meselor în familie, pentru mulți vârstnici singuri aceste zile sunt printre cele mai grele din an. Singurătatea doare, lipsurile și sentimentul de abandon se simt mai apăsat tocmai atunci când toți ceilalți par... Post-ul De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: After the COVID‑19 pandemic, thousands more kindergarteners faced developmental challenges
mix_items.summary: Children with special needs often experience a range of early developmental challenges that can affect their readiness for learning and their participation in school.

mix_items.title: A new way to strengthen the body's defense against respiratory viruses
mix_items.summary: Researchers have discovered a new method to boost the body's natural ability to fight respiratory viruses, including respiratory syncytial virus (RSV), one of the leading causes of severe lung infections worldwide.

mix_items.title: Dementia risk factors may depend on which country you live in
mix_items.summary: A new study led by Curtin University has revealed millions of dementia cases across the Western Pacific Region could potentially be prevented by implementing country-specific strategies to address key risk factors such as education access, obesity and mental health.

mix_items.title: This German dialect leaves AI baffled, exposing a digital language blind spot
mix_items.summary: How well do language models understand Meenzerisch, the dialect spoken in the German city of Mainz? A research team led by Johannes Gutenberg University Mainz (JGU) has now investigated this question for the first time. Meenzerisch still shapes regional language culture today and is known across Germany through the satirical carnival speeches in the famous Mainz Fastnacht tradition. The study's findings, recently presented at the 2026 Language Resources and Evaluation Conference (LREC) in Palma de Mallorca, show that current Artificial Intelligence (AI) models have great difficulty understanding the dialect correctly.

mix_items.title: Benefits of using a Bayesian statistical framework for replicating research
mix_items.summary: The "replication crisis" refers to a problem in the sciences where findings from previous experiments don't hold up when studies are repeated. It is a particular issue for those in the behavioral sciences and experimental psychology; studies that should hold up, don't, and researchers have struggled with the vast task of both confirming what is known and trying to glean new and accurate information. The concerns ramped up in the 2010s, when repeated experiments to corroborate published psychological studies ended up failing to replicate, adding to concerns about how studies are conducted and published in the first place.

mix_items.title: How a 4,000-year-old city defied history's 'rules' by becoming more equal as it became more successful
mix_items.summary: For decades, historians have generally agreed that the progress of small villages as they evolved into cities came at the price of widening inequality. A small group of leaders, kings and priests, would inevitably seize control of the wealth and the gap between rich and poor would grow.

mix_items.title: Molecular net boosts the power of natural biopesticides
mix_items.summary: Scientists at VIB and Vrije Universiteit Brussel have uncovered a previously unknown mechanism that helps a widely used biological pesticide become more effective. The study, published in Nature Communications, reveals how bacteria produce ultra-strong protein fibers that form a molecular net, trapping infectious spores and toxins into a sticky film that enhances their ability to kill insect pests.

mix_items.title: Elderly people are more sexually active than most people think
mix_items.summary: Sexuality is an important part of life—even when we grow old. The idea that desire disappears with age is a myth that needs to be debunked, argues a psychologist who has researched older adults' sex lives.

mix_items.title: Economists solve a mystery involving international trade and competition from China
mix_items.summary: Economists have identified—and resolved—a seeming paradox regarding how competition from China affects the price and volume of products that are exported from other countries into the United States. The findings shed new light on the complex dynamics of international trade and how the effects of trade competition vary drastically for poor nations compared to their wealthy counterparts.

mix_items.title: Protecting the brain: How clumps of protein might actually be saving your cells
mix_items.summary: What if the very structures we thought were destroying the brain are actually trying to save it? A new study reveals that protein clumps, long considered toxic markers of diseases like Huntington's, act as a vital "quarantine" system to keep neurons alive. By identifying the protein ATF3 as the master switch that builds these shields, researchers have uncovered a natural defense mechanism that shifts the focus from destroying these clumps to strengthening the brain's internal armor. This discovery offers a fresh perspective on how we might one day treat neurodegenerative disorders by working with the body's own survival strategies.

mix_items.title: How a shifting Nile landscape shaped the rise of the ancient empire of Kush in Sudan
mix_items.summary: When I first became co-director of an archaeological project at Jebel Barkal in northern Sudan in 2018, I was amazed by the site's pyramids, temples and palaces. It had been an urban center in the ancient empire of Kush, which dominated the Nile Valley off and on for more than 2,000 years, from 2000 B.C.E. to 350 C.E.

mix_items.title: BMI alone does not fully capture health risks linked to obesity, new study finds
mix_items.summary: Obesity is commonly diagnosed using BMI, but this approach has several limitations. Researchers at Lund University and AstraZeneca show that integrating measurements such as body fat percentage and waist circumference captures disease risks missed by BMI alone.

mix_items.title: 'Jumping gene' helps explain elevated pancreatic cancer risk in French-Canadians
mix_items.summary: Researchers at McGill University have discovered a centuries-old genetic mutation that helps to explain why some French‑Canadians in Quebec are at an elevated risk of pancreatic cancer. Until quite recently, standard genetic tests have not been able to identify this "jumping gene" cause.

mix_items.title: Grokipedia selectively draws on more-right leaning news sources, says new study
mix_items.summary: A large-scale analysis of Grokipedia, the world's first AI-written encyclopedia, has found that while many Grokipedia articles closely resemble their Wikipedia counterparts, a substantial subset diverged markedly in style, sourcing and political leaning.

mix_items.title: Moon-Venus Conjunction
mix_items.summary: The Moon and Venus, center, are seen in conjunction above the Washington Monument, Monday, May 18, 2026, as viewed from the Mary W. Jackson NASA Headquarters Building in Washington. The Moon and Venus look close together because they line up from our point of view on Earth. In reality, they are separated by millions of […]

mix_items.title: Therapy at your fingertips: New study finds AI could transform mental health care
mix_items.summary: A new study from Reichman University, published in JAMA Network Open, has found that an AI-based conversational support platform can significantly reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression, form a meaningful therapeutic alliance with users, and provide round-the-clock emotional support. The study is titled "Efficacy of a Conversational AI Agent for Psychiatric Symptoms and Digital Therapeutic Alliance: A Randomized Clinical Trial."

mix_items.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
mix_items.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized features, such as stubby arms and ant-eating adaptations. Weighing under two pounds, the dinosaur is one of the smallest known from South America.

mix_items.title: Scientists finally solve the mystery of yeast’s tiny centromeres
mix_items.summary: Scientists have uncovered how brewer’s yeast developed its unusually tiny centromeres, the DNA regions that guide chromosome separation during cell division. By studying related yeast species, researchers found centromeres that appear to represent evolutionary halfway points. These structures seem to have formed from retrotransposons—mobile “jumping genes” in the genome. The discovery shows how DNA once considered genomic junk can be transformed into essential chromosome machinery.

mix_items.title: Light-guided evolution creates proteins that can switch, sense, and compute
mix_items.summary: Researchers have created a method called optovolution that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. By engineering yeast cells so their survival depended on proteins switching states at the right time, scientists could rapidly select the best-performing variants. The technique produced new light-sensitive proteins that respond to different colors and improved optogenetic systems. It even evolved a protein that behaves like a tiny logic gate, activating genes only when two signals are present.

mix_items.title: Scientists discover hidden species among Borneo’s “fanged frogs”
mix_items.summary: DNA is revealing that many animals once thought to be a single species may actually be several hidden ones. But research on Bornean fanged frogs shows the line between species can be blurry—an important challenge when deciding what wildlife needs protection most.

mix_items.title: Lake Coatepeque
mix_items.summary: Set amid El Salvador’s modern, active volcanic landscape, tranquil blue waters fill a caldera formed by ancient eruptions. The post Lake Coatepeque appeared first on NASA Science .

mix_items.title: Scientists stunned to find signs of ancient life in a place no one expected
mix_items.summary: While exploring ancient seabeds in Morocco, scientists discovered strange wrinkle-like textures in deep-water sediments that shouldn’t have been there. These structures are usually made by sunlight-loving microbial mats in shallow waters. But the rocks formed far below the reach of light, suggesting a different explanation. Evidence points to chemosynthetic microbes—organisms powered by chemical reactions—creating the mats in the dark depths of an ancient ocean.

mix_items.title: Scientists finally reveal why mint feels cold
mix_items.summary: Scientists have revealed how the body’s microscopic cold sensor, TRPM8, detects both chilly temperatures and the cooling effect of menthol. The discovery finally shows how the sensation of “cool” works at the molecular level—and could inspire new treatments for pain and eye disorders.

mix_items.title: Bird droppings helped build one of ancient Peru’s most powerful kingdoms
mix_items.summary: New research suggests seabird guano helped transform the Chincha Kingdom into one of the most prosperous societies in ancient Peru. Chemical clues in centuries-old maize show farmers fertilized their crops with guano gathered from nearby islands, dramatically boosting yields in the desert landscape. The resulting agricultural surplus fueled trade, population growth, and regional influence.

joke_ro.text: Azi am fost productiv: am mutat haosul dintr-un colt in altul.

satire_ro.title: Supărat că parcul din Berceni nu-i va mai purta numele, Trump nu-şi mai botează câinele Băluţă
